title: support host processes with `podman kube play`
body:
/kind feature

Currently, `podman kube play` is functionally very similar to `docker-compose up`.

When developing locally, I don't want to just run containers. I want to run host processes too. I.e. a process that run directly on the host. These are usually the app I'm working on. These are typically much faster to run because the host is just faster. I'm running directly on the silicon with faster access to CPU and memory as well as disk, build caches etc. 

We're talking 10x to 100x faster.

These host process would not have an image, such `command`. The would inherit the host environ and hosts file.

comments.body: Not sure what you are asking, but in general containers are just host processes with different kernel settings.  So they run just as fast as normal processes launched outside of containers.
